title: VSAS-Bench: Real-Time Evaluation of Visual Streaming Assistant Models
gist: Apple researchers introduce VSAS-Bench, a benchmark for real-time visual streaming assistants that measures proactiveness and consistency beyond offline video understanding, and show conventional VLMs adapted to streaming (e.g. Qwen3-VL-4B) can outperform purpose-built streaming VLMs.

insights.claim: Streaming vision-language models must be judged on metrics beyond pure video understanding, including proactiveness (timeliness of responses) and consistency (robustness of responses over time), which offline benchmarks ignore.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Defines the evaluation axes that will matter for real-time visual assistants (e.g. always-on camera agents), shaping how the field measures progress.
insights.quote: including proactiveness, which reflects the timeliness of the model’s responses, and consistency, which captures the robustness of its responses over time
insights.locator: the performance of a streaming VLM depends on additional metrics

insights.claim: VSAS-Bench provides temporally dense annotations with over 18,000 annotations across diverse input domains and task types, plus standardized synchronous and asynchronous evaluation protocols.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: A new standardized benchmark can become a reference point for vendors marketing real-time multimodal assistants.
insights.quote: VSAS-Bench features temporally dense annotations with over 18,000 annotations across diverse input domains and task types

insights.claim: Conventional VLMs can be adapted to streaming settings without additional training and these adapted models outperform recent purpose-built streaming VLMs.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Suggests specialized streaming-VLM architectures may be unnecessary, undercutting the moat of streaming-native model builders.
insights.quote: conventional VLMs can be adapted to streaming settings without additional training, and demonstrate that these adapted models outperform recent streaming VLMs

insights.claim: Qwen3-VL-4B surpasses Dispider, the best streaming VLM on the benchmark, by 3% under the asynchronous protocol.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Concrete head-to-head: a general open VLM beating a streaming-specialist is a signal that off-the-shelf models close the gap cheaply.
insights.quote: Qwen3-VL-4B surpasses Dispider, the best streaming VLM on our benchmark by 3% under asynchronous protocol

insights.claim: The accuracy-latency trade-off in streaming VLMs is governed by key design factors including memory buffer length, memory access policy, and input resolution.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Identifies the practical knobs engineers must tune for real-time visual assistants on constrained hardware.
insights.quote: analyzing the accuracy–latency trade-off under key design factors such as memory buffer length, memory access policy, and input resolution
insights.locator: Using this framework, we conduct large-scale evaluations
